topic: Area of Study 4: Western Classical Tradition since 1910
subject: music

items.q: Is all classical music written since 1910 atonal?
items.correct: No - tonal and modal styles continued throughout the century: neoclassicism, folk-influenced music and minimalism mostly keep a sense of key or centre.
items.misconception: After 1910 every serious composer abandoned keys completely.
items.random_wrong: After 1910 composers were only allowed to write in C major.
items.why: Atonality was one strand among many; treating it as universal makes students mis-describe tonal twentieth-century set works.

items.q: Does minimalist music stay exactly the same all the way through?
items.correct: No - it changes constantly but gradually, adding notes, shifting patterns out of phase or slowly altering the texture.
items.misconception: Minimalism is boring because literally nothing changes.
items.random_wrong: Minimalist pieces must last under one minute.
items.why: The interest of minimalism lies in perceiving slow processes of change; listening for the gradual transformations is the analytical skill being tested.

items.q: Does 'expressionist' music sound gentle and romantic because it is expressive?
items.correct: No - expressionism uses extreme dissonance, angular lines and violent contrasts to express raw inner states.
items.misconception: Expressionism means sweetly expressive, lyrical music.
items.random_wrong: Expressionism is music written to accompany painting classes.
items.why: The art-history label misleads: expressionism deliberately distorts and intensifies, which is why its sound world is harsh rather than soothing.

items.q: Which composer is a leading figure of minimalism?
items.why: Correct answer: Steve Reich. “Edward Elgar” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Neoclassicism is best described as what?
items.correct: A twentieth-century revival of eighteenth-century clarity and forms
items.why: Correct answer: A twentieth-century revival of eighteenth-century clarity and forms. “Music written for silent films” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Bela Bartok is famous for doing what?
items.correct: Collecting folk songs and using them in his compositions
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Collecting folk songs and using them in his compositions. “Inventing the electric guitar” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: A group of adjacent notes played together
items.why: Correct answer: A group of adjacent notes played together. “A very fast scale” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which decade did minimalism first emerge in the USA?
items.why: The real figure is 1960 decade. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
